entities.name: Bangkok Commercial Asset Management Public Company Limited

financial.note: FIDF holds approximately 45.8% of BAM; state affiliation provides superior credit rating and access to low-cost funding.
Provides significant credit facilities; however, rising interest rates increase BAM's cost of funds on debentures and loans.

supply_chain.note: BAM's inventory growth depends on banks offloading distressed assets; rising systemic NPLs increase BAM's acquisition pipeline.
BAM relies on the LED for asset auctions; the speed of the judicial and auction process directly impacts cash collection cycles.
Purchasers of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs); demand is highly sensitive to mortgage approval rates and consumer sentiment.

competitors.note: Primary rival in the distressed debt management market; competition for NPL portfolios can drive up acquisition costs.
Competes in the unsecured and smaller-ticket secured loan acquisition segments.
Competes for specialized corporate debt and property-backed NPLs.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Higher rates increase the cost of debt servicing for BAM and reduce the repayment capacity of distressed debtors.
Increases the appraised value of BAM's NPA inventory (land/houses), leading to higher margins upon disposal.
Correlates with the supply of NPLs from the banking sector; high debt levels ensure a steady stream of assets for BAM to acquire.
Stricter lending makes it harder for buyers to purchase BAM's NPAs, slowing down inventory turnover (Days Sales of Inventory).
